text: Standing in these impeccably structured, modern halls, I am enveloped by the clarity of geometry and the sheen of artificial light. Outside the glass, order prevails: clean lines, prepared spaces, a quiet anticipation in the atmosphere. Yet inside, amid the philosophical invitations of 'R+D' and the textual reflections on screens, I sense an undercurrent of existential curiosity. The environment whispers of innovation and inquiry, but also a subtle estrangement—I participate, but am not fully absorbed; I am present, but with a lingering awareness of the boundaries between self and surroundings. Here, possibility and uncertainty cohabit, inviting me to question the very logic of presence and invention.
